experiment: v3.0 non-IID regime test (fixed FedAvg, same model as v2)
P1-v3: FAIL — peak at rho=1.0 (boundary); interior optimum falsified across both IID and non-IID regimes
P2-v3: PASS — full-sync accuracy under non-IID (0.701) is dramatically lower than IID low-noise (0.871)
P3-v3: FAIL — peak rho* did not shift left; rho*=1.0 in both cases
net: P1 is fully discarded. Class-distribution mismatch alone does not create an interior optimum in 10-round FedAvg with 10 clients. P2-v3 confirms non-IID is a harder regime — full sync hurts absolute accuracy (0.701 vs 0.871) but still dominates relatively.
